name: redis-cloud
description: Inspect Redis Cloud subscriptions and databases across Pro and Essentials plans

envVars.name: REDIS_CLOUD_API_KEY
envVars.description: Redis Cloud account key (sent as x-api-key)

envVars.name: REDIS_CLOUD_SECRET_KEY
envVars.description: Redis Cloud user secret key (sent as x-api-secret-key)

tools.name: List Subscriptions
tools.description: List Redis Cloud Pro subscriptions in the account

historicalSummary.omitted: cloud provider deployment details and pricing fields

tools.name: List Essentials Subscriptions
tools.description: List Redis Cloud Essentials (fixed plan) subscriptions in the account

tools.name: List Databases
tools.description: List databases in a Redis Cloud Pro subscription

offset.description: Number of databases to skip for pagination

tools.name: Get Database
tools.description: Get configuration and status of a database in a Pro subscription

tools.name: List Essentials Databases
tools.description: List databases in a Redis Cloud Essentials subscription

subscriptionId.description: Essentials subscription ID (from List Essentials Subscriptions)

prompts.title: Database inventory
prompts.prompt: List my Redis Cloud subscriptions and the databases in each, with their status and memory limits.

prompts.title: Check a database
prompts.prompt: Get the details of my main Redis Cloud database and summarize its configuration.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Redis Cloud API Setup

steps.title: Enable the API
steps.description: Sign in to Redis Cloud (https://cloud.redis.io) as an account owner and go to Access Management → API Keys. Free accounts can sign up and create an Essentials subscription to test with. Enable the API if prompted.

steps.title: Copy the account key
steps.description: The account key identifies your account and is shown on the API Keys page. Add it to your .env as REDIS_CLOUD_API_KEY.

steps.title: Create a user secret key
steps.description: On the same page, add a new API user key. The secret is shown only once — copy it and add it as REDIS_CLOUD_SECRET_KEY.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Subscriptions (Pro) or List Essentials Subscriptions (fixed plans).

notes: Requests authenticate with two headers: x-api-key (account key) and x-api-secret-key (user secret key) — both env vars are required
Pro and Essentials subscriptions live under different paths: /v1/subscriptions vs /v1/fixed/subscriptions
The API is rate limited to 400 requests per minute per account key
